top_5_cases.headline: Pending System Approval for High-Value International Flights
top_5_cases.full_reasoning: The flights for Hesham Ali to Casablanca were booked based on an email stating that system approval would be shared later. No formal ERP approval was found in the provided emails, leaving a significant amount at risk.

evidence.quote: Approved. I will share with you the system approval once received. Please proceed so that we secure the ticket.

top_5_cases.headline: Hotel Expense Misclassified as Flight Ticket
top_5_cases.full_reasoning: An invoice row for 11800 SAR was billed under a flight/ticket GL, but the corresponding email explicitly states this amount was for a hotel booking.

top_5_cases.headline: Registration Expense Misclassified as Flight Ticket
top_5_cases.full_reasoning: Multiple invoice rows totaling 11615 SAR were billed under a flight/ticket GL, but the corresponding email indicates these amounts were for event registration.

top_5_cases.headline: Significant Amount Mismatch for International Flights
top_5_cases.full_reasoning: The invoice amounts for Sultan Abu Doghmeh and Mostafa Amer (4619.99 SAR each) significantly exceed their respective approved amounts (3300.0 SAR and 3100.0 SAR).

evidence.pointer: RE_ Approved_ Personal Contribution Approval Requested for Sultan Mohammad Abu Doghmeh (1000995) on 2026-04-21 by Sultan Mohammad Abu Doghmeh.msg

top_5_cases.headline: Meeting Room Expense Misclassified as Flight Ticket
top_5_cases.full_reasoning: Three invoice rows totaling 6625 SAR were billed under a flight/ticket GL for a 'MEETING ROOM 27 APR', which is a clear misclassification of expenses.

key_themes: Frequent misclassification of hotel, registration, and meeting room expenses as flight tickets.
Significant discrepancies between approved amounts in ERP and actual invoiced amounts.
Multiple instances of duplicate billing for the same sponsorship events.
Several high-value international flights booked without formal ERP approval.
